docs.title: OpenWeather API Documentation
docs.text:
This guide was written for anyone who has never used an API before and wants to retrieve real, live weather data without needing a coding background.

The official OpenWeather documentation is written for developers. This guide is written for everyone else — if you can use a web browser, you can follow this guide.

docs.title: What You Will Learn
docs.text:
• How to create an OpenWeather account and get an API key
  • How to make your first API request using Postman
  • How to read and understand the response data
  • How to recognise and fix common errors

docs.title: What You Need
docs.text:
• A web browser
  • A free OpenWeather account
  • A free Postman account
  • No coding experience required

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Get the current weather for London: 

GET https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather
  ?q=London
  &appid=YOUR_API_KEY
  &units=metric

A successful response looks like this: 

{
    "name": "London",
    "main": {
        "temp": 7.69,
        "feels_like": 5.1,
        "humidity": 93
    },
    "weather": [{ "description": "overcast clouds" }],
    "cod": 200
}

docs.title: How This Guide Is Organised
docs.text:
Section What It Covers Authentication How to get and use your API key Endpoints The URL structure and available parameters Reading the Response What each field in the response means Error Handling What goes wrong and how to fix it Quick Reference A one-page summary of everything

Tip

Never used an API before? Start with the Authentication page — it takes less than 5 minutes to set up.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Every request to the OpenWeather API requires an API key. This key identifies who you are and tracks your usage.

docs.title: Getting Your API Key
docs.text:
1. Go to https://openweathermap.org
  2. Click Sign Up and create a free account
  3. Once logged in, click your username and select My API Keys
  4. Copy your default key or generate a new one

Warning

New API keys take up to 2 hours to activate. If you get a 401 error straight away, wait and try again.

docs.title: Using Your API Key
docs.text:
Add your key to every request using the appid parameter: 

https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q=London&appid=YOUR_API_KEY

docs.title: Keeping Your Key Safe
docs.text:
• Never share your API key publicly
  • Never paste it into a website or social media post
  • Store it in a notes app or password manager
  • If you think it has been compromised, delete it and generate a new one

docs.title: Current Weather
docs.text:
The main endpoint for retrieving current weather data for any location. 

GET https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description Example q Yes (or lat/lon) City name q=London lat Alternative to q Latitude coordinate lat=51.51 lon Alternative to q Longitude coordinate lon=-0.13 appid Yes Your API key appid=abc123 units No Temperature unit units=metric lang No Response language lang=en

docs.title: Units
docs.text: Value Temperature Wind Speed standard Kelvin m/s metric Celsius m/s imperial Fahrenheit mph

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather
  ?q=Prague
  &appid=YOUR_API_KEY
  &units=metric

Tip

Use metric for Celsius or imperial for Fahrenheit. If you leave units out, temperatures default to Kelvin.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: When something goes wrong, the OpenWeather API returns a short JSON response with an error code and a message explaining what happened.

docs.title: 401 — Unauthorized
docs.text:
{
    "cod": 401,
    "message": "Invalid API key."
}

Cause: Your API key is missing, incorrect, or not yet activated.

Fix: Double-check your API key. New keys take up to 2 hours to activate.

docs.title: 404 — Not Found
docs.text:
{
    "cod": "404",
    "message": "city not found"
}

Cause: The city name you provided does not exist or is misspelled.

Fix: Check your spelling or use latitude and longitude coordinates instead.

docs.title: 429 — Too Many Requests
docs.text:
{
    "cod": 429,
    "message": "Your account is temporary blocked due to exceeding of requests limitation of your subscription type."
}

Cause: You have exceeded your plan's rate limit.

Fix: Wait a moment before trying again. Consider upgrading your plan if this happens frequently.

docs.title: 500 — Server Error
docs.text:
Cause: Something went wrong on OpenWeather's end.

Fix: Wait 30 seconds and try again. If it persists, check the OpenWeather status page.

docs.title: Error Code Summary
docs.text:
Code Meaning Action 200 Success Process the data normally 401 Unauthorized Check your API key 404 Not found Check the city name 429 Rate limit exceeded Wait and retry 500 Server error Retry after 30 seconds

Tip

Always check the cod field in every response before processing the data — even if the HTTP status looks fine.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before you begin, make sure you have everything listed below. Each item is free and takes less than 5 minutes to set up.

docs.title: What You Need
docs.text: Requirement Why You Need It Where To Get It Web browser To access Postman and OpenWeather Already on your computer OpenWeather account To get your API key openweathermap.org Postman account To send API requests postman.com Internet connection To call the API —

docs.title: No Coding Required
docs.text: This guide uses Postman — a visual tool that lets you send API requests without writing any code. If you are comfortable using a web browser, you can follow this guide.

docs.title: Time Required
docs.text: Task Estimated Time Create OpenWeather account 5 minutes Get your API key 2 minutes Set up Postman 3 minutes Make your first request 2 minutes Total ~12 minutes

docs.title: Before You Continue
docs.text:
Make sure you can answer yes to all of these:

  • [ ] I have a web browser open
  • [ ] I have created an OpenWeather account
  • [ ] I have copied my API key somewhere safe
  • [ ] I have created a Postman account

Warning

New OpenWeather API keys take up to 2 hours to activate. Create your account and key first, then come back to continue when the key is ready.

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: A handy summary of everything you need to use the OpenWeather API.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather

docs.title: Full Request Example
docs.text:
https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather
  ?q=London,uk
  &appid=YOUR_API_KEY
  &units=metric

docs.title: Required Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description q City name (e.g. London or London,uk) appid Your API key

docs.title: Optional Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description Default units metric / imperial / standard standard lang Language for descriptions en lat & lon Coordinates instead of city name —

docs.title: Status Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 200 Success 401 Invalid API key 404 City not found 429 Rate limit exceeded 500 Server error

docs.title: Useful Links
docs.text:
• OpenWeather API Docs
  • Your API Keys
  • OpenWeather Pricing

Tip

Bookmark this page for quick access whenever you are working with the OpenWeather API!

docs.title: Reading the Response
docs.text: A successful request returns an HTTP 200 status code with a JSON body containing the current weather data.

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
    "name": "London",
    "main": {
        "temp": 7.69,
        "feels_like": 5.1,
        "humidity": 93
    },
    "weather": [
        {
            "main": "Clouds",
            "description": "overcast clouds"
        }
    ],
    "wind": {
        "speed": 4.12
    },
    "visibility": 10000,
    "cod": 200
}

docs.title: Key Fields Explained
docs.text:
Field Description Example name City name "London" main.temp Current temperature 7.69 main.feels_like Perceived temperature 5.1 main.humidity Humidity percentage 93 weather[0].description Weather condition "overcast clouds" wind.speed Wind speed in m/s 4.12 visibility Visibility in metres 10000 cod Status code (200 = success) 200

Tip

The dt field returns the time of the data as a Unix timestamp. You can convert it to a readable date using any online Unix converter.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: A guide to the most common problems you may encounter when using the OpenWeather API and how to fix them.

docs.title: My key returns a 401 error immediately after creating it
docs.text: New API keys take up to 2 hours to activate. Wait and try again.

docs.title: I copied my key correctly but still get 401
docs.text: Check for accidental spaces before or after the key when pasting it into Postman. Even one extra space will cause a 401 error.

docs.title: The API returns the wrong city
docs.text:
Some city names exist in multiple countries. Add a country code to be specific: 

?q=London,uk
?q=Paris,fr
?q=Prague,cz

docs.title: I get a 404 for a city I know exists
docs.text:
Try using coordinates instead of the city name: 

?lat=50.088&lon=14.421

docs.title: The temperature looks wrong
docs.text:
Check your units parameter:

units value Temperature format metric Celsius imperial Fahrenheit standard (default) Kelvin

docs.title: Some fields are missing from my response
docs.text: Not all fields are always present. For example rain and snow only appear when precipitation is actually occurring.

docs.title: I get no response at all
docs.text:
• Check your internet connection
  • Make sure you are using https:// not http://
  • Check that your firewall is not blocking the request

docs.title: Still Stuck?
docs.text:
• Check the OpenWeather FAQ
  • Check the OpenWeather API Status
